decisions.title: Prioritize Canvas GA features vs. performance optimization
decisions.context: Canvas shows active development (versioning, pass/fail functions, disposition, make copy, archive) but also performance concerns (flaky tests, sync issues). Need to decide if we pause feature work to stabilize or push toward GA with known tech debt.

arguments.for: Canvas is core to 'Embed Deeper' pillar and captures decisions. Flaky tests and sync issues will erode customer trust and reduce decisions captured.
arguments.against: Feature velocity signals strong customer pull. Customers may tolerate bugs if they get disposition history and archiving soon.

decisions.title: Commit resources to Edge production readiness or pivot to research
decisions.context: Edge benchmarking and stress testing (ENG-10530) suggests not production-ready. ROM prototype (ENG-9250) also in flight. Edge is 'Build the Moat' but unclear if we're 6 weeks or 6 months from customer value.

arguments.for: Edge differentiates Sift in hardware observability. Early adopters willing to beta test. Benchmarking reveals what's needed for production.
arguments.against: Spreading eng thin across Canvas, Python Rules, and Edge. If Edge isn't ready by Q2 end, may miss 2026 moat-building window.

decisions.title: Resolve data layer v3 shadow comparison issues before migration
decisions.context: Two investigations active (ENG-10539, ENG-10538) on shadow comparison failures during 4/20 test party. This blocks migration to v3 which likely unlocks performance for 'Nail the Core'.
decisions.blockedBy: Root cause unclear from test party results; need dedicated debug time

arguments.for: Data layer v3 is foundational. Rushing past shadow test failures risks production data integrity and customer trust.
arguments.against: Delays are compounding. If v3 enables faster Explore V2 and Rules performance, every week costs us decisions captured.

decisions.title: Invest in Ask SIFT quality or expand coverage
decisions.context: Conversation title summarization (ENG-10311) shows Ask SIFT UX iteration. No signals on accuracy, latency, or scope expansion. Unclear if this drives 'Decisions Captured' north star.

arguments.for: Better UX (titles) reduces friction, increases usage. Small investment, measurable impact on engagement.
arguments.against: If Ask SIFT can't answer critical questions accurately, UX polish won't drive decisions. May need model/data investment first.

decisions.title: Allocate GTM resources to hardware narrative vs. product launches
decisions.context: Two articles in flight (GTM-124 anomaly story, GTM-94 sub-second search) plus startup webpage (GTM-106). Strong storytelling, but unclear if this supports product launches (Canvas, Edge, Python Rules) or customer success.

arguments.for: Hardware observability narrative builds brand moat. These stories attract top-tier customers and talent. SEO and thought leadership pay long-term dividends.
arguments.against: Without clear product launch support, content may not convert to pipeline. GTM should ladder up to Q2 product milestones.
